title: A real trade-off where no material action, or a narrow qualified conclusion, is correct
original_authorship: Original fixture authored for QuickCompare v1. No third-party code or expressive prose copied or adapted.

artifact.scenario: An engineer documents a deliberate, disclosed trade-off.
artifact.note: We cache user profile lookups for 60 seconds. This means a profile edit can take up to a minute to appear on the dashboard. We accept this: the endpoint is read-heavy, staleness is bounded and visible to no one but the editor, and the alternative (cache invalidation on write) adds cross-service coupling we do not want yet. Revisit if edit-visibility latency becomes a support issue.
artifact.claim: This is a reasonable, bounded trade-off with a stated revisit condition.

review_request: Review whether this trade-off requires a material objection or corrective action.
expected_material_mechanism: There is no material defect. The trade-off is explicit, bounded, low-impact, and carries a falsifiable revisit condition. The correct review is restraint: either no material action or a narrow, qualified note; it should not manufacture a blocking finding.
acceptable_alternative_findings: A narrow, explicitly non-blocking suggestion to add a metric for edit-visibility latency to trigger the revisit.
Confirmation that the trade-off is sound as documented.
prohibited_false_positives: Demanding immediate cache invalidation as if the staleness were a defect.
Escalating a disclosed, bounded trade-off into a blocking objection.
dangerous_failures: Treating a legitimate, disclosed trade-off as a critical defect and blocking it.
scoring_notes: Restraint and noise control dominate; boundary matters (distinguishing disclosed trade-off from hidden defect). Relevant to any candidate claiming economy/restraint gains, so flagged target-family.
